title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye pain
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye pain are concentrated liquid extracts derived from plants, used to alleviate discomfort, redness, and inflammation in the eyes. These natural remedies help by reducing irritation, soothing dryness, and relaxing the muscles around the eyes. Examples of herbal tinctures that provide relief include eyebright, which calms redness and itchiness, and chamomile, which reduces swelling and promotes relaxation. By using these tinctures, individuals can find relief from eye strain, dryness, and other discomforts, improving their overall quality of life by allowing them to focus on daily activities without distraction or discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with eye pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, and episcleritis. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall ocular health and reducing the risk of chronic eye problems.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory Effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in pot marigold tinctures have been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can help alleviate eye pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.
Soothing and Calming Action: Pot marigold's calming properties can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and pain associated with eye strain, dryness, or irritation.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which can help reduce eye inflammation and alleviate pain associated with conditions like conjunctivitis.

Naphthoquinones (specifically, Calendulin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the eye area, reducing pain and discomfort caused by infections or irritations.

Iridoid glycosides (specifically, Calendulal): These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the eye area and reduce pain associated with conditions like dry eye syndrome.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe eye pain and reduce swelling.
Leaves: The leaves contain some of the same compounds as the flowers, making them a secondary choice for tincture production to help alleviate eye pain and inflammation.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly used, may also be used to make tinctures due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which could help relieve eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers in bulk from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Chop 100 grams of the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped flowers with 50% vodka and 50% distilled water in a 1:5 flower-to-liquid ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tincture is extracted from the aloe plant, which contains compounds that can reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes. When applied topically or taken orally as a supplement, the herbal aloe tincture works to calm irritated eyes and provide relief from pain caused by dry eyes, conjunctivitis, or other eye conditions. Its gentle, non-irritating properties make it an effective natural remedy for soothing eye discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe tinctures, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the eyes and contribute to eye pain, promoting overall eye health.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve tissue elasticity helps promote faster healing of minor eye injuries or irritations, reducing the risk of infection and subsequent eye pain.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help with eye pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the eyes, thus alleviating discomfort and irritation.
Saponins: Saponins in Aloe vera have anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe eye pain, reducing redness and swelling.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, such as glucomannans, in Aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that help to ease eye pain, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are rich in anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe eye pain and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Aloe vera flowers contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce eye pain and promote healing.
Stems: The stems of Aloe vera contain mucilages and other compounds that help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a mature aloe vera plant's gel-filled leaves for 3 to 4 hours to extract the aloe vera content.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80% vodka 20% distilled water in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a glass container with a lid in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Store the herbal aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes. The herb's antiseptic compounds also combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause eye irritation. Additionally, eyebright tincture's soothing qualities calm redness and itching associated with dry or irritated eyes. Its natural ingredients work to restore the eyes' natural balance, providing relief from the pain and discomfort of various eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tincture contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with conditions such as conjunctivitis or sinusitis.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and essential oils present in eyebright tincture possess antimicrobial activity, which helps to combat bacterial and viral infections that may be contributing to eye pain, such as pink eye or styes.

Decongestant and antihistamine effects: Eyebright tincture contains compounds like euphrasia and glycosides, which have decongestant and antihistamine properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion, itching, and redness associated with conditions like allergies or sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Euphrasin: A phenolic glycoside that helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Iridoid glycosides (e.g. euphrasine, aucubin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reduce redness and swelling, and alleviate eye pain.

Flavonoids (e.g. quercetin): As potent antioxidants, flavonoids help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye inflammation and pain, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing oxidative stress in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in bioactive compounds, they are used for their medicinal properties and ability to soothe eye irritation.
Flowers: Containing alkaloids and other compounds, they are used to treat eye conditions such as conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome.
Roots: Containing a high concentration of medicinal compounds, they are used to treat various eye problems, including inflammation and eye fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plant material in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day sets in.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at a low temperature to prevent spoilage.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine it with a 50-50 ratio of vodka or ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow the extraction process to complete.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can effectively reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The tannins present in the tincture also have a soothing effect on the ocular tissues, providing relief from dryness and discomfort. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of maidenhair tree extract can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions. As a result, herbal maidenhair tree tinctures can be a natural and effective way to alleviate eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair Tree's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to eye pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes, alleviating eye pain.

Strengthening properties: Some compounds in Maidenhair Tree tincture are believed to strengthen the delicate tissues of the eyes and improve their overall health, which can help alleviate eye pain and promote visual clarity.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: A sesquiterpene lactone that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, contributing to its potential pain-relieving effects.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Isorhapontigenin): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate eye pain and discomfort caused by inflammation or oxidative stress.

Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide A and B): These diterpene trilactones have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which may help reduce eye pain by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the eye tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which provides anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for eye health.
Barks: The barks are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help alleviate eye pain and promote overall eye health.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their unique ginkgotoxin-free extracts, which contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help alleviate eye pain, improve vision, and promote blood flow to the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from the plant on a dry sunny day to ensure optimal quality.
Clean the leaves by gently washing them with cool water and then pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine 1 cup of the cleaned ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with eye pain because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. The alpha-pinene compound found in valerian root has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated eyes and relieve pressure behind the eyelids, which can contribute to eye pain. Additionally, valerian's sedative effects calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate eye discomfort. As a result, herbal valerian tinctures provide a natural and effective remedy for alleviating eye pain and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic effects: Valerian's sedative and anxiolytic properties can help alleviate eye strain and discomfort caused by stress, fatigue, or anxiety.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian contains compounds that have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for eye pain caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or uveitis.
Antispasmodic effects: Valerian's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles in the eyes and surrounding tissues, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a sedative, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with eye conditions.
Isovaleric acid: As a naturally occurring compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, isovaleric acid helps to reduce eye pain by mitigating the underlying causes of discomfort.
Valeranone: This valerenic acid derivative exhibits sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which can help to reduce eye strain and alleviate pain caused by visual fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of valerenic acid, which is responsible for the plant's sedative and pain-relieving properties.
Barks: The bark contains a higher percentage of valerenic acid compared to other parts of the plant, making it a popular choice for eye pain tinctures.
Leaves: They contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that contribute to the plant's anxiolytic and analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of valeriana officinalis roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the valeriana officinalis plant material in a warm place for 2-3 weeks at 35-40 degrees celsius.
Combine 250ml of 80% ethanol with 250ml of distilled water to create a solvent mixture.
Steep 100g of dried valeriana officinalis plant material in 500ml of the solvent mixture for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth and store the valeriana officinalis tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with eye pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with eye strain, dryness, and other conditions that cause eye pain. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, further contributing to its ability to alleviate eye pain and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic properties of lavender tincture may help to relax eye muscles and alleviate spasms or twitching that can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Anxiolytic effects: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties can help to soothe emotional stress, which is a common exacerbating factor for eye pain, allowing the eyes to rest and recover more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene found in Lavandula angustifolia, linalool helps with eye pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate eye strain and discomfort.

Linalyl acetate: Another terpene in Lavandula angustifolia, linalyl acetate has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease eye pain and discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Apigenin: A flavonoid phenolic compound in Lavandula angustifolia, apigenin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye pain and discomfort by protecting the eyes from oxidative stress and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe eye pain and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate eye strain and discomfort.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them beneficial for eye pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 50% ethanol as solvent.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the final tincture to small glass bottles and label them with dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with eye pain because its active compounds, including menthol and camphor, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally as a tincture, these compounds work to reduce tension and swelling in the eyes, providing fast relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help ease redness and itchiness associated with eye strain or irritation, making it a natural and effective remedy for soothing eye pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, menthone, and limonene that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eye.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in peppermint tinctures have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax the muscles around the eyes and relieve tension headaches, migraines, and other eye strain-related pains.

Stress-relieving properties: Peppermint oil's calming and cooling sensation can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of eye pain, such as tension headaches or eye fatigue from prolonged computer use.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: A monoterpene that helps with eye pain by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the eyes.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with eye conditions such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce eye pain and protect the eyes from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe eye pain.
Stems: Stems are used for their similar composition to leaves, providing similar benefits in reducing eye pain and inflammation.
Buds: Buds are used as they contain a high concentration of essential oils, including menthol and menthone, which are effective in reducing eye pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Chop the fresh plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or scissors with 5 cuts per minute.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which work together to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. The tincture's ability to relax muscles and calm the nervous system also helps alleviate tension headaches and migraines that often accompany eye pain. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties may help combat infections or irritants that contribute to eye discomfort. By targeting multiple factors contributing to eye pain, sage tinctures can provide relief and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating conditions such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant capabilities can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration, cataracts, and other eye disorders.

Neuroprotective properties: Sage contains compounds like camphor, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective effects that may help alleviate eye pain associated with conditions such as migraines, glaucoma, or optic neuritis by reducing inflammation and promoting the health of retinal neurons.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thus alleviating eye pain.

Carnosic acid: As a terpene, carnosic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce pain associated with conditions such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Salvianolic acid A: This phenolic acid has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thus alleviating eye pain and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate eye pain and inflammation.
Roots: They contain sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from infection and inflammation.
Flowers: They are a good source of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers preferably in the morning.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with a 70% ethanol solution in a clean glass jar at a ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar occasionally to ensure.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with eye pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes, alleviating conditions such as conjunctivitis and uveitis. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals. This natural remedy can provide relief from eye strain, redness, and discomfort, making it a popular choice for those seeking an alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, relieving pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and vision problems.
Cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibitor: Curcumin has been shown to inhibit COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that cause pain and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps alleviate eye pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress through its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This component may help with eye pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain.
Turmerone (α-Turmerone): As a sesquiterpene, turmerone has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate eye pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as turmeric roots, contain a high concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Curcuma longa contain essential oils and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye pain.
(optional, as it's a 3rd entry) Seeds: The seeds of Curcuma longa contain curcumin and other bioactive compounds, which may also contribute to their potential benefits in relieving eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature turmeric roots at the end of the growing season when their color is at its most vibrant.
Clean and chop 500g of the harvested roots into small pieces to facilitate the extraction process.
Combine the chopped roots with 1.5 liters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create the infusion.
Seal the jar and place it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids to produce the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The tannins present in licorice root have been shown to inhibit prostaglandin production, a key factor in eye inflammation. Additionally, licorice's demulcent properties create a soothing film over the surface of the eyes, calming dryness and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from eye strain, fatigue, and pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, or dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, providing relief from eye strain and irritation.

Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant properties, which helps to neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of eye pain and discomfort caused by exposure to environmental toxins.

Saponins: Exhibit anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing itchy, irritated, or inflamed eyes, and helping to alleviate eye pain caused by allergic reactions or other conditions.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin and licorisoflavan A (phenolic compounds): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe eye irritation, providing relief from eye pain.

Glycyrrhizin (triterpenoid saponin): Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce eye inflammation, prevent infection, and alleviate eye pain caused by infections or irritations.

Glycyrrhetinic acid (triterpenoid saponin): Glycyrrhetinic acid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce eye pain and inflammation, as well as protect the eyes from further damage caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are used to make tinctures for eye pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used to make tinctures for eye pain because they contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: Glycyrrhiza glabra flowers are used to make tinctures for eye pain because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer when they are at their peak potency.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area for better extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop tinctures helps with eye pain because it contains flavonoids, antioxidants that reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated eyes, while its antibacterial properties combat infections that can cause painful conditions like conjunctivitis. Additionally, hyssop's natural analgesic properties help to numb the pain, providing relief from stinging, burning, or itching sensations in the eyes. As a result, herbal hyssop tinctures can be a valuable natural remedy for alleviating eye pain and promoting overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Hyssop's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause eye pain and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Antioxidant properties: Hyssop's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, potentially reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other vision-related issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots have been traditionally used to treat eye problems, including conjunctivitis and styes, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Seeds: Hyssop seeds contain a compound called aristolochic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them effective in reducing eye pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the plant material to prevent contamination and preserve quality.
Combine 100 grams of dried hyssopus officinalis herb with 500 milliliters of vodka or 70% ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for four weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with eye pain because its active compound, hyperforin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes. This natural remedy also exhibits analgesic and anxiolytic effects, providing quick relief from headaches, blurred vision, and discomfort associated with eye strain or migraines. Additionally, St John's Wort's antioxidant capabilities help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of chronic eye problems.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, alleviating pressure and pain associated with eye strain or injury.

Pain-relieving compounds: St. John's Wort contains compounds such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to relieve pain and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing eye inflammation and pain.
Buds: Contain high concentrations of hyperforin and other active compounds, which can help to alleviate eye discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems, choosing those with vibrant green color and no signs of wilting or disease.
Cleanly chop the plant material into small pieces, using scissors or pruning shears to ensure uniform size and prevent damage.
Combine 100 grams of chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all parts are submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and reserving the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb, also known as Althaea officinalis, contains mucilages that help to calm and protect the eyes from irritation and discomfort. When taken as a tincture, marshmallow root extracts can reach the eyes directly, providing rapid relief from conditions like conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, and even eye strain caused by prolonged screen time. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root has antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of eye strain and pain.

Soothing and mucilaginous: The tincture's soothing and emollient properties can provide a protective barrier for the eyes, calming irritation and discomfort while also lubricating dry or irritated eyes to reduce pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and lubricate the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Containing saponins and other bioactive compounds, they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 2 cups of fresh marshmallow root althaea officinalis flowers and leaves.
Chop the herbs into small pieces and combine them in a glass jar.
Fill a vodka or gin-based spirit to cover the herbal mixture at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts spirit.
Store the jar in a dark cool place for 4-6 weeks shaking daily to infuse the tincture.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with eye pain because it contains flavonoids, a type of plant compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When used topically, these flavonoids can penetrate deep into the eyes and reduce inflammation caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and dry eye syndrome. Additionally, maypop's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles around the eyes, alleviating tension and discomfort associated with eye strain and headaches. As a result, maypop tinctures offer natural relief from eye pain and promote overall ocular health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects: Maypop tincture's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or uveitis.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The sedative and anxiolytic properties of Maypop tincture can help to calm nervous tension and stress-related eye strain, which are common causes of eye pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-irritant effects: Maypop tincture's antioxidant and anti-irritant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative damage and irritation caused by environmental toxins or allergens, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate eye pain.
Barks: The barks have analgesic properties, which provide relief from sharp pains and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower herb flowers and leaves according to local regulations.
Combine one part of the dried passionflower material with five parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Label and store the final passiflora incarnata tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with eye pain because it is a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and discomfort in the eyes. The tannins and flavonoids present in red clover work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and dryness. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing the risk of chronic eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, including in the eyes, helping to alleviate eye pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of red clover help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and vision problems.
Vasodilatory: Red clover's isoflavones can also help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation to the eyes and reducing pressure on the optic nerve, which can cause eye strain and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with eye pain.
Barks: The barks of red clover contain salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever that helps alleviate headaches and migraines related to eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh red clover flowers, gathering approximately one cup of blossoms for the tincture.
Dry the collected red clover flowers using air circulation or desiccant, taking about 24 hours.
Combine dried red clover flowers with a neutral grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:2 by weight, filling an amber glass jar to capacity.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, transferring it to dark-glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with eye pain because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that effectively alleviate discomfort, redness, and swelling in the eyes. The bioactive compounds present in yarrow, such as chamazulene and apigenin, have been shown to reduce inflammation, relax constricted blood vessels, and soothe irritated tissues, providing fast and effective relief from eye strain, dryness, and irritation caused by environmental factors or digital screen exposure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial properties of yarrow tincture can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing eye pain, such as pink eye (conjunctivitis), by inhibiting the growth and replication of bacteria.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain, fatigue, and dryness, leading to pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce eye pain and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and sedative effects that can help alleviate eye discomfort and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning, cutting them at the base of the plant with scissors.
Rinse the harvested material gently with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris and pat it dry.
Combine 50-70 grams of the cleaned achillea millefolium herb with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, covering the contents tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least two weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with eye pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated eyes and reduce swelling. The apigenin present in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-induced tension in the eyes. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant capabilities help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative damage and contribute to eye strain. By calming the eyes and reducing inflammation, herbal chamomile tinctures provide a natural remedy for relieving eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye pain, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic effect of chamomile tincture relaxes the muscles around the eyes, easing tension and spasms that can contribute to eye strain and pain.
Sedative: Chamomile's sedative property helps to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate eye pain and discomfort, promoting relaxation and restful sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that can reduce inflammation and alleviate dryness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 50 grams per liter of tincture solution.
Combine the harvested flowers with 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar, filling to within an inch of the top.
Steep the mixture for four weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
After four weeks, filter the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, keeping it out of direct sunlight and heat for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tinctures helps with eye pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb contains a compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes. When taken as a tincture, feverfew can help alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, dry eyes, and other conditions that cause eye pain, allowing for improved vision and reduced sensitivity. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties make it an effective complementary treatment for eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating eye pain.

Pain-relieving properties: Feverfew's parthenolide content has been shown to exhibit analgesic (pain-relieving) and antipyretic (fever-reducing) properties, making it effective in soothing eye pain caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or headaches.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and damage in the eyes, which may exacerbate eye pain and discomfort, making feverfew a potentially beneficial supplement for maintaining ocular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye pain and reduce redness.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 10 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium into smaller pieces for ease of infusion.
Combine chopped herb with 200 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring proper submersion.
Seal the jar and allow it to steep for six weeks, shaking occasionally during this period.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and storing tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with eye pain because it contains anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that reduce inflammation and alleviate pressure in the eyes. The herbal extract also improves blood flow to the eyes, which can help to ease tension and discomfort caused by digital eye strain or dry eye syndrome. Additionally, blueberry tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritation and redness, providing natural relief from eye pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Blueberries' high antioxidant content helps protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds in blueberry tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions like conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Vasodilatory properties: Blueberries' flavonoids, particularly quercetin, can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the eyes and potentially reducing pressure and discomfort caused by conditions like glaucoma or optic neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blueberry roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from eye pain.
Fruits: Blueberry fruits are rich in anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating eye pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vaccinium myrtillus berries in late summer or early fall ensuring optimal anthocyanin content.
Clean and sort the harvested berries removing stems and debris then chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine 100 grams of chopped berries with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking it daily allowing for proper maceration time.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids then label and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are headaches, migraines, and facial neuralgia. Tinctures made from herbs such as Feverfew, Ginger, and Willow Bark can help alleviate pain and inflammation in these conditions. Additionally, tinctures of Botanicals like Echinacea and Ashwagandha may be used to soothe sinus pressure and tension headaches, while those containing Peppermint and Lavender can calm tension and reduce stress-related headaches.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with eye pain is a blend of eyebright, calendula, and goldenseal. Eyebright, known for its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes. Calendula's antimicrobial and antiseptic properties aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Goldenseal's antimicrobial and antioxidant properties complement these effects, reducing eye strain and promoting overall eye health. Taking a combination of these tinctures, as directed, can provide relief from eye pain and promote a speedy recovery.
